title: Responses WebSocket Session
interaction_flow: Caller → responses_websocket_session: async with responses_websocket_session(api_key, base_url, websocket_base_url, ...)
responses_websocket_session → ResponsesWebSocketSession: __post_init__ → _validate_provider_alignment() checks MultiProvider.openai_provider is same provider instance
Caller → ResponsesWebSocketSession: session.run(starting_agent, input, **kwargs)  OR  session.run_streamed(...)
ResponsesWebSocketSession → ResponsesWebSocketSession: _prepare_runner_kwargs('run', kwargs) — injects run_config, raises if run_config already present
Runner → OpenAIResponsesWSModel: get_response(system_instructions, input, model_settings, tools, output_schema, handoffs, tracing, previous_response_id, conversation_id, prompt)
OpenAIResponsesWSModel → OpenAIResponsesModel: _build_response_create_kwargs(system_instructions, input, model_settings, tools, output_schema, handoffs, stream=True, ...) → create_kwargs dict
_ensure_websocket_connection → _ensure_websocket_connection: check cached connection reusability (_is_websocket_connection_reusable) — reuse if OPEN + same identity + same event loop
_ensure_websocket_connection → WebSocketConnection: _open_websocket_connection(ws_url, headers, connect_timeout) if not reusable
_await_websocket_with_timeout → WebSocketConnection: send frame to OpenAI Responses API (WS)
_await_websocket_with_timeout → WebSocketConnection: recv() — raw frame bytes/str from API
_iter_websocket_response_events → _iter_websocket_response_events: if terminal event (response.completed / response.failed / response.incomplete) → return (stop loop)
[non-stream path] _fetch_response → _fetch_response: consume all events; on ResponseCompletedEvent capture event.response
_fetch_response → _fetch_response: if final_response is None after loop → raise RuntimeError
get_response → Runner: return ModelResponse(output=response.output, usage=Usage(...), response_id=response.id, request_id=None)
MultiProvider → OpenAIResponsesWSModel: close() — increments _ws_client_close_generation, drops WS connection
key_relationships: ResponsesWebSocketSession frozen dataclass wraps OpenAIProvider + RunConfig(MultiProvider)
OpenAIResponsesWSModel overrides _fetch_response to always use websocket (_iter_websocket_response_events), ignoring HTTP path
_iter_websocket_response_events depends_on _await_websocket_with_timeout for all network I/O phases
ResponsesWebSocketSession delegates to Runner.run / Runner.run_streamed with injected RunConfig
MultiProvider routes model IDs by prefix, uses openai_prefix_mode ('alias'|'model_id') and unknown_prefix_mode
error_handling: TimeoutError raised per phase: 'request lock wait', 'connect', 'send', 'receive' — wraps asyncio.TimeoutError
ResponsesWebSocketError raised on error/response.error event frames
Pre-event disconnect wraps as RuntimeError with _openai_agents_ws_replay_safety='safe'|'unsafe'
On CancelledError or non-terminal GeneratorExit: force-abort connection (_force_abort_websocket_connection) + clear state
On other exceptions mid-stream: drop connection (_drop_websocket_connection)
get_retry_advice: stateful requests (previous_response_id/conversation_id) are not retried if response started
connection_lifecycle: Connection cached in self._ws_connection; reused across turns if URL+headers identity matches and connection.state=='OPEN'
Each request serialized via asyncio.Lock (_ws_request_lock, per event loop)
close() force-aborts if lock held mid-request; otherwise gracefully drops
_ws_client_close_generation prevents retry on stale close-triggered disconnect
important_notes: WS transport always streams; get_response() internally consumes the stream to produce a single Response
No request_id on WS responses (no x-request-id header on WS upgrade; ModelResponse.request_id=None)
Caller must drain streamed iterators before context exits to avoid force-closing the shared WS connection
run_config must NOT be passed to session.run()/run_streamed() — session injects it automatically
openai_prefix_mode='model_id' for endpoints that expect namespaced model IDs literally
